metadata.official_name: University of Notre Dame du Lac

strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Notre Dame du Lac is an elite, private Catholic research university (R1) known for its rigorous academics, deep commitment to faith and service, and fiercely loyal alumni network. It offers a highly residential campus life and need-blind, full-need financial aid for all students [cite: 3, 7].
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic review with a focus on academic rigor, character, leadership, and alignment with the university's Catholic mission and service ethos [cite: 8, 9].

analyst_takeaways.insight: Notre Dame's admit rate has fallen below 12%, making it highly selective, though legacy applicants historically enjoy significantly higher acceptance rates [cite: 1, 2].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must present near-perfect academic credentials alongside profound extracurricular impacts; applying REA or having legacy status offers a notable mathematical advantage.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Notre Dame recently expanded its need-blind admissions to international students and implemented the Pathways program, eliminating tuition for families earning under $150,000 [cite: 2].
analyst_takeaways.implication: The university is exceptionally accessible for high-achieving low- and middle-income students, globally.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The Mendoza College of Business strictly caps its enrollment to approximately 550 students per class [cite: 4, 10].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Prospective business students must indicate this major on their application to secure pre-approval, as internal transfers are highly restricted.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Highly spirited, residential community centered around dorm life and athletics (especially football). A distinct lack of Greek life is replaced by tight-knit residence hall communities where 80% of undergrads live [cite: 6, 7].
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: A self-contained, 1,250-acre campus in a suburban setting near South Bend, Indiana, with reasonable access to Chicago for travel and networking [cite: 2, 11].
campus_dynamics.research_impact: An R1 research institution with very high research activity, heavily emphasizing undergraduate research opportunities across all disciplines [cite: 12, 13].

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional through the 2024-2025 cycle (Class of 2029). The university will return to requiring SAT or ACT scores beginning with the 2025-2026 application cycle (Class of 2030) [cite: 14].

longitudinal_trends.notes: Record high applications and record low acceptance rate of 9% [cite: 11].
Highest yield rate since the advent of the Common App [cite: 15, 16].
Waitlist admits dropped heavily in subsequent years [cite: 16, 17].

gpa.notes: Specific 4.0 scale GPAs are not disclosed, but 92% of enrolled students were in the top 10% of their high school class [cite: 18].

testing.policy_note: A non-submission penalty was practically observed, as only one-third of non-submitters received admission [cite: 11].

colleges.name: Mendoza College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students certain they want to study business must indicate this on their application to secure pre-approval, as internal transfers are strictly capped to maintain a class size of ~550 [cite: 4, 10].
colleges.student_archetype: Driven, networking-oriented, ethically grounded future corporate leaders.

majors.name: Finance
majors.special_requirements: Must complete Foundations of Finance with a C or higher [cite: 19].

electives: FIN 40260 Python and Data Applications in Finance

majors.notes: Finance is the most popular major at the university [cite: 20].

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Strong analytical thinkers interested in ethical technological innovation.

majors.name: Computer Science

core_courses: CSE 40113 Design and Analysis of Algorithms

majors.notes: Available as both a B.S. in the College of Engineering and a B.A. in the College of Arts and Letters [cite: 21, 22].

colleges.name: College of Arts and Letters
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students seeking a foundational liberal arts education routed in Catholic intellectual tradition.

majors.name: Political Science
majors.notes: Highly popular major providing strong pathways to law and government service [cite: 23, 24].

majors.name: Economics
majors.notes: Often used as a strong alternative major for students interested in business but not admitted to Mendoza [cite: 25, 26].

colleges.name: College of Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Aspiring healthcare professionals and scientific researchers.

majors.name: Biology

core_courses: BIOS 11174 Research Experience in Biology Laboratory

majors.notes: Extensive pre-health track with significant undergraduate research options [cite: 27, 28].

application_deadlines.notes: Restrictive Early Action: non-binding but applicants cannot apply Early Decision to other private universities [cite: 11].

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires 3 to 4 short-answer essays (approx 150 words) to showcase personality, resilience, and fit with the university's Catholic mission [cite: 29, 30].

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Religious affiliation and legacy status are uniquely heavily weighted compared to peer institutions [cite: 1, 30].

major_selection_tactics: Apply directly to the Mendoza College of Business if you intend to study business; the internal transfer route is heavily capped and restricted.
If you are undecided, applying to the College of Arts and Letters or College of Science allows for very fluid major switching later.
Consider your high school coursework context—admissions expects you to have taken the most rigorous courses available in the field you claim to want to study [cite: 31].
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Economics and the Science-Business program serve as robust alternative majors for students who are not pre-approved for Mendoza but still want to enter finance or consulting [cite: 10].

scholarships.name: Stamps Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers tuition and fees, plus a $12,000 enrichment fund for research/travel, and pairs the student with a faculty mentor [cite: 32, 33].

ap_policy.general_rule: Credit is awarded for scores of 4 and 5.
ap_policy.usage: While credits are accepted, they generally do not apply to university, college, major, or minor requirements and function primarily as general elective credits (with some exceptions like writing courses) [cite: 34, 35].

ib_policy.general_rule: Credit awarded for scores of 6 and 7 on Higher Level exams [cite: 34].

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer students must provide 24 transferable credits with a highly competitive college GPA (often 3.75+) [cite: 36].

what_it_takes: Demonstrating a deep commitment to service and alignment with the university's Catholic values
Resilience and evidence of being a 'force for good'
A high degree of intellectual discipline shown through course rigor
common_activities: Varsity sports (one-third of men were high school team captains)
Leadership in clubs or student government
essay_tips: Shine on the personal essays by showcasing your unique personality and fit for the university.
Highlight how your life experiences have fostered compassion, integrity, and a drive to positively influence the world.
Be authentic and let your sense of humor and resilience show [cite: 29, 30].
red_flags: Lack of demonstrated service or moral grounding
Taking 'easy' courses to pad GPA rather than challenging oneself
Applying to Mendoza as a backup plan without genuine interest
insights: No formal interviews are offered, placing extreme weight on essays and letters of recommendation [cite: 9].
Demonstrated interest is not tracked or considered in the admissions process [cite: 30].
